channel.title: Libertas - Freedom Tech Signals
channel.description: Curated insights on freedom technology: Bitcoin, privacy tools, censorship resistance, and digital sovereignty.

item.title: CBP Signs Deal with Clearview AI for Border Surveillance Face Recognition
item.description: US Border Patrol intelligence units will deploy Clearview AI's facial recognition system built on billions of scraped internet images for 'tactical targeting' operations.

item.title: DHS Plans Unified Biometric Platform After Dismantling Privacy Reviews
item.description: Department of Homeland Security seeks to merge face and fingerprint databases into single platform while removing centralized privacy oversight and facial recognition limits.

item.title: EFF Calls on Tech Giants to Resist DHS's Unlawful Surveillance Subpoenas Targeting Activists
item.description: EFF and ACLU are demanding major tech companies require court orders before complying with DHS subpoenas that target First Amendment activities, after DHS withdrew challenged subpoenas rather than defend them in court.

item.title: Cameroon Police Seize Journalists' Equipment, Threaten Sources Over US Deportation Story
item.description: Cameroonian police detained 4 journalists for 5 hours, seized phones/laptops, and threatened to 'crush' them while investigating secret US migrant deportations, raising source protection concerns.

item.title: Citizen Lab Reports on Transnational Repression Methods to EU Parliament
item.description: Citizen Lab researchers present findings on transnational repression tactics within EU to Parliament committee, recommending EU-wide TNR definition and central knowledge hub for counter-strategies.

item.title: Pentagon Threatens AI Company Anthropic Over Surveillance Restrictions
item.description: Department of Defense threatening to label Anthropic a "supply chain risk" after company refused to lift restrictions on surveillance and autonomous weapons use of their AI.

item.title: UN Rules Pakistani Human Rights Defender's Detention Violates International Law
item.description: UN Working Group found Pakistan's imprisonment of Pashtun human rights defender Idris Khattak unlawful, calling for immediate release after 5+ years detention for documenting enforced disappearances.

item.title: New Attacks on Fiat-Shamir Zero-Knowledge Proofs Threaten Privacy Systems
item.description: Researchers found practical attacks against Fiat-Shamir-based zero-knowledge proof systems, potentially compromising privacy protocols that rely on these cryptographic foundations.

item.title: Signal Launches Built-in Censorship Circumvention with TLS Proxy Support
item.description: Signal deployed built-in censorship circumvention features including TLS proxy support to help users bypass government blocks and maintain private communications.

item.title: OONI Documents Media Censorship During India-Pakistan Conflict
item.description: OONI and Digital Rights Foundation documented coordinated media censorship by both India and Pakistan during their May 2025 conflict, with governments blocking news outlets citing 'disinformation' and 'hate speech.'

item.title: Signal Announces New Features Roll Call Blog Series for App Improvements
item.description: Signal launched a periodic blog series to highlight new features and quality of life improvements, emphasizing their commitment to truly private messaging without data harvesting for ads or AI training.

item.title: India Civil Society Updates OONI Website Censorship Test Lists
item.description: The Bachchao Project and OONI collaborated to update the website list used for monitoring internet censorship in India, strengthening civil society's ability to track government blocking.

item.title: Federal Agents Clone Marine Veteran's Phone Without Warrant After Minneapolis Shooting
item.description: Federal ICE/Border Patrol agents detained Marine veteran Steven Saari for 6+ hours, forced biometric collection, and cloned his phone without a warrant after he observed the Alex Pretti shooting scene in Minneapolis.

item.title: FCC Chairman Urges Broadcasters to Join 'Pledge America Campaign' for Pro-America Programming
item.description: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr launched a campaign asking broadcasters to voluntarily air patriotic programming including daily Pledge of Allegiance and civic education content for Trump's America 250th celebration.

item.title: Senegal Implements Multi-Phase Internet Shutdowns During Political Protests
item.description: NetBlocks documented systematic internet censorship in Senegal during 2023 protests, including social media blocks, mobile data curfews, and platform-specific restrictions targeting opposition to Ousmane Sonko's sentencing.

item.title: Signal Rolls Out Secure Backups Feature After Years of User Requests
item.description: Signal launches opt-in secure backups in Android beta, addressing the top user request to preserve encrypted message history when phones are lost or damaged.

item.title: Iran Conducts Mass Arrests, Enforced Disappearances Following January 2026 Massacre
item.description: Iran arrested tens of thousands following January 8-9 massacres, conducting mass enforced disappearances, torture, and death sentences in coordinated crackdown to suppress dissent.

item.title: Zimbabwe Detains Journalist for Week Over Online Corruption Report
item.description: Zimbabwean authorities detained Bulawayo24 journalist Gideon Madzikatidze for a week on cyberbullying and broadcasting charges after he published online corruption allegations against waste management company.

item.title: 17 Orgs Fight FTC's Retaliatory Investigation Into Media Matters
item.description: Coalition led by The Intercept filed amicus brief to stop FTC's allegedly retaliatory investigation into Media Matters after they published critical Trump administration coverage.

item.title: Senator Blumenthal Opens Binance Investigation Over Iran Sanctions Evasion Reports
item.description: US Senator Richard Blumenthal launched an inquiry into Binance following reports the exchange may have facilitated Iran-linked sanctions violations.

item.title: Signal Announces New President Role to Focus on Sustainability and Strategy
item.description: Signal appointed a new President on September 12 to focus on long-term sustainability, strategy, and communications for the encrypted messaging platform.

item.title: Ukrainian Authorities Investigated for Surveilling 10 Anti-Corruption Journalists
item.description: Ukrainian police opened criminal investigation after NABU uncovered alleged surveillance of 10 journalists covering corruption, with their personal data found on seized devices from a criminal organization involving government officials.

item.title: Israel Blocks 37 Aid Groups Over Staff Data Requirements, Citing Security
item.description: Israeli authorities are barring 37 international NGOs from Gaza/West Bank operations after they refused new registration requirements demanding staff lists and biodata, citing violations of humanitarian neutrality principles.

item.title: UK Issues Secret Order for Apple iCloud Encryption Backdoor, Global Impact Feared
item.description: UK government secretly ordered Apple to backdoor end-to-end encrypted iCloud backups globally. Apple disabled Advanced Data Protection for UK users rather than comply, highlighting encryption policy precedent risks.

item.title: FBI Asset Helped Run Dark Web Market for Years, Approved Fentanyl Sales
item.description: FBI secretly controlled a staffer at Incognito dark web market who approved sales of fentanyl-laced drugs, revealing extent of law enforcement infiltration of anonymous marketplaces.

item.title: Pakistan Implements Total Internet Shutdowns During Political Arrests
item.description: Pakistan disrupted social media platforms and imposed total mobile internet shutdowns following former PM Imran Khan's arrest, with NetBlocks confirming multi-day blackouts affecting fundamental rights.

item.title: Russia's Media Crackdown: 27 Jailed, 355 Labeled 'Foreign Agents', 25K+ Sites Blocked
item.description: CPJ documents unprecedented Russian media repression since 2022: 27 journalists imprisoned, 355 outlets labeled 'foreign agents', and over 25,000 websites blocked, creating urgent need for censorship-resistant tools.

item.title: EFF Defends Section 230 as Essential Protection for Online Speech and Freedom Tech
item.description: EFF argues Section 230's immunity protections are critical for free expression online, warning that weakening them would force platforms to pre-censor content and chill speech across the internet.

item.title: EFF Hosts Livestream on Fighting Flock ALPR Surveillance in Cities
item.description: EFF is hosting a livestream Feb 19 on fighting Flock automated license plate readers, featuring case studies of successful campaigns that blocked surveillance deployments across US cities.

item.title: Amazon Ring Expands Biometric Surveillance Network with AI-Powered Pet Tracking
item.description: Amazon Ring's new 'Search Party' feature uses AI biometrics to scan neighborhood camera networks, expanding surveillance infrastructure with law enforcement partnerships despite privacy violations.

item.title: Thai Court Sentences 4 Free Speech Activists to 32 Months for Royal Criticism
item.description: Thai activists including Pimsiri Petchnamrob received 32-month prison sentences for criticizing the monarchy under lese majeste laws, highlighting severe censorship repression in Thailand.

item.title: Ethiopia Blocks Major Social Media Platforms During Religious Protests
item.description: Ethiopia restricted Facebook, Telegram, TikTok and YouTube on state ISP Ethio Telecom during anti-government protests over Ethiopian Orthodox Church tensions, with NetBlocks confirming circumvention via VPN services.

item.title: North Korea Escalates Tech Surveillance and Info Controls Targeting Youth
item.description: North Korea's 2026 Party Congress coincides with intensified crackdowns on tech-savvy youth accessing foreign media, with new laws criminalizing information access and public executions for sharing content.

item.title: Access Now Launches Digital Security Webinar Series for Civil Society
item.description: Access Now's Digital Security Helpline launches 'Safe and Strong' webinar series targeting spear-phishing threats against activists, journalists, and civil society with expert guidance from Citizen Lab and security researchers.

item.title: OONI Launches 2025 Fundraising Campaign Amid Global Censorship Surge
item.description: Internet freedom measurement organization OONI launches fundraising campaign as they document increasing censorship of social media, messaging apps, and news sites across 10+ countries in 2024.

item.title: FBI Counterterrorism Unit Targets Climate Activists Under Trump's NSPM-7 Directive
item.description: FBI counterterrorism agents visited former Extinction Rebellion member's home for questioning, marking escalation in Trump administration's targeting of activist groups as domestic terrorists under NSPM-7.

item.title: OONI Establishes Foundation, Advances Global Censorship Detection Tools in 2024
item.description: OONI launched a new foundation and significantly upgraded its censorship detection toolkit with OONI Run v2, multiplatform apps, and enhanced data analysis capabilities to support global internet freedom monitoring.

item.title: Brazil Blocks Telegram Over Refusal to Provide User Data on Far-Right Groups
item.description: Brazil restricted Telegram on major ISPs after the messaging platform refused court orders to provide user data from alleged far-right groups planning school attacks.

item.title: EFF Backs State Laws to Sue Federal Agents for Constitutional Violations
item.description: EFF supports California S.B. 747 and similar state laws enabling lawsuits against federal agents who violate constitutional rights, as Supreme Court has gutted federal protections while ICE/CBP violate filming rights.

item.title: OONI Develops Anonymous Credential System for Censorship Measurement Privacy
item.description: OONI is integrating anonymous credentials into OONI Probe to increase trust in censorship measurements while preserving user privacy and anonymity.

item.title: Pakistan Jails Human Rights Lawyers 17 Years for Tweets Criticizing Military
item.description: Pakistan sentenced two human rights lawyers to 17 years in prison for tweets criticizing military abuses in Balochistan, using cybercrime laws to prosecute digital dissent.

item.title: Iran Implements Complete Internet Shutdown During Protests, 2,403+ Killed
item.description: Iran shut down all internet and mobile connectivity nationwide on Jan 8, 2026 amid currency protests. Over 2,400 protesters killed, 18,000+ arrested as authorities jam even satellite internet to conceal violence.

item.title: Tails 7.0 RC2 Released with Debian 13, Updated Privacy Tools
item.description: Tails privacy OS releases second release candidate based on Debian 13, updating core freedom tech tools like Tor, Electrum Bitcoin wallet, and OnionShare file sharing.

item.title: OONI and Digitally Right Document Network Shutdowns During Bangladesh Protests
item.description: OONI collaborated with local digital rights group Digitally Right to document internet shutdowns and censorship during recent Bangladesh protests, providing crucial evidence of network interference.

item.title: CPJ Report: 58 of 59 Palestinian Journalists Report Torture in Israeli Detention
item.description: Committee to Protect Journalists releasing report Feb 19 documenting systematic torture claims from 58 of 59 Palestinian journalists detained by Israel since Oct 2023.

item.title: Tails 7.4 Adds Persistent Language Settings, Drops BitTorrent Support
item.description: Tails 7.4 introduces persistent language/keyboard settings and removes BitTorrent downloads due to security concerns with v1 to v2 transition.

item.title: Signal Announces Quantum-Resistant Protocol Upgrade (PQXDH)
item.description: Signal Protocol introduces PQXDH upgrade, adding quantum-resistant cryptography layer to protect against future quantum computers that could break current encryption.

item.title: OONI Launches Enhanced Censorship Detection Platform, Seeks Community Feedback
item.description: OONI has released major platform upgrades including new censorship findings tools and improved methodologies, and is requesting community feedback to evaluate impact.

item.title: Silent.Link: Bitcoin-Native eSIM Service Bypasses SIM-Swap Attacks and State Censorship
item.description: Silent.Link offers privacy-focused eSIM data/SMS services paid with Bitcoin, eliminating SIM-swap risks while bypassing state firewalls in China and Dubai's VoIP restrictions.

item.title: Open Measurement Gathering Hosts Public AMA on Internet Censorship Detection Tools
item.description: Key internet measurement organizations (OONI, Censored Planet, IODA, Measurement Lab) held public AMA sharing project updates and future plans for detecting censorship and network interference.

item.title: Meta Considers Facial Recognition for Smart Glasses Despite $7B Privacy Settlement History
item.description: Meta is reportedly planning facial recognition for smart glasses, timing launch during political distractions. EFF warns of mass surveillance risks and billions in legal costs.

item.title: Gabon Implements Indefinite Social Media Shutdown Amid Anti-Government Protests
item.description: Gabon's communications authority ordered indefinite social media blocking of Facebook, WhatsApp, TikTok, Instagram and YouTube during ongoing protests, with #KeepItOn coalition demanding immediate restoration.

item.title: OONI Documents Ongoing X/Twitter Blocking Across Tanzania Networks
item.description: Technical measurements confirm X (Twitter) has been blocked across major Tanzanian ISPs since May 2025, with network-level censorship affecting multiple autonomous systems.

item.title: Journalist Safety Expert Highlights Digital Security Tools for High-Risk Environments
item.description: Free Press Unlimited's Pablo Arcuri outlines digital security priorities for journalists, emphasizing phone protection and tools like VPNs while connecting online disinformation to physical violence.

item.title: UK Court Orders Saudi Arabia to Pay £3m to Dissident Targeted with Pegasus Spyware
item.description: UK court awarded £3m to Saudi dissident Ghanem Al-Masarir after Citizen Lab proved his phones were infected with NSO Group's Pegasus spyware by Saudi operators, setting precedent for transnational surveillance accountability.

item.title: Uganda Faces Election Internet Shutdown Threat Despite Coalition Warning
item.description: Digital rights coalition warns Uganda's government against internet shutdowns ahead of 2026 elections, citing escalating media crackdown and concerning signals including VPN threats and Starlink blocking directive.

item.title: MTN Congo Accused of Enabling State Surveillance in Gang Crackdown
item.description: Access Now alleges MTN Congo sent mass SMS bounty messages for security forces targeting suspected gang members, potentially violating human rights commitments and data protection laws.

item.title: Kimwolf Botnet Sybil Attack Disrupts I2P Anonymity Network
item.description: The massive Kimwolf IoT botnet accidentally crippled I2P's anonymous communications network by flooding it with 700k infected devices attempting to use it as fallback C2 infrastructure.

item.title: Tails 7.4.2 Emergency Release Fixes Critical Kernel Vulnerabilities
item.description: Tails issued emergency update fixing Linux kernel vulnerabilities that could allow privilege escalation and potential deanonymization of users.

item.title: Proton Guide: Technical Tools to Resist AI Training Data Scraping
item.description: Privacy company Proton outlines practical methods for creators to protect their work from AI training scraping, including pixel-level cloaking tools, data poisoning techniques, and GDPR opt-outs.

item.title: Mauritania Imposed 6-Day Mobile Internet Shutdown After Prison Break
item.description: Mauritania cut mobile internet nationwide for 6 days following a violent prison escape, affecting businesses and demonstrating how infrastructure-level shutdowns bypass VPN protections.

item.title: Ethiopia Revokes Reuters Credentials After Sudan Training Camp Investigation
item.description: Ethiopia revoked Reuters journalists' accreditation following report alleging secret UAE-financed camp training Sudan's RSF fighters, part of broader media crackdown ahead of June elections.

item.title: Wisconsin VPN Ban Bill Passes Assembly, Senate Vote Imminent
item.description: Wisconsin's S.B. 130/A.B. 105 effectively bans VPNs and mandates invasive age verification for websites, passed State Assembly and faces Senate vote tomorrow.

item.title: OONI Publishes Complete Security Audit Report for Censorship Measurement Tools
item.description: OONI publicly released a comprehensive security audit report of its internet censorship measurement platform, conducted by Radically Open Security with OTF support.

item.title: New York Proposes Mandatory 3D Printer Surveillance for Firearm Detection
item.description: New York's budget bill would require all 3D printers sold in the state to include 'blocking technology' that scans files for potential firearm blueprints and refuses to print flagged items.

item.title: OONI Launches Community Survey to Improve Censorship Measurement Tools
item.description: OONI, the global internet censorship measurement network, is surveying its 50+ partner organizations and hundreds of thousands of users to improve their tools and outreach.

item.title: OONI Explorer Launches Thematic Censorship Tracking Pages
item.description: OONI Explorer released new dedicated pages tracking global censorship of social media, news media, and circumvention tools with charts and data-driven reports.

item.title: EFF Releases Guide for Journalists to Investigate Police Surveillance Tech
item.description: EFF, Center for Just Journalism, and IPVM released 'Selling Safety' report to help journalists cut through police tech marketing hype and report accurately on surveillance tools' costs, effectiveness, and privacy impacts.

item.title: North Korea Escalates Surveillance and Punishment of Tech-Savvy Youth
item.description: Human Rights Watch reports North Korea has intensified surveillance targeting young people with technical skills, with punishments including execution for accessing foreign media or sharing content.

item.title: Rights Groups Demand Accountability from AppLogic Networks for Egypt Censorship Infrastructure
item.description: Human rights coalition demands transparency from AppLogic Networks (formerly Sandvine) over withdrawal from Egypt, where its deep packet inspection tech blocked 630+ websites including independent media outlets.

item.title: Tor Project Launches 'Free the Internet' Fundraising Campaign with Matched Donations
item.description: Tor Project announces fundraising campaign highlighting real-world censorship resistance use cases in Turkmenistan, Russia, and Egypt, with donations matched 1:1 up to $250K.

item.title: Philippines 'Red-Tags' Environmental Groups, Freezes Bank Accounts of Solar/Disaster Relief NGOs
item.description: Philippine government systematically labels environmental defenders as 'terrorists,' freezing bank accounts of NGOs providing solar power and disaster relief to rural communities.

item.title: OONI Advances Probe Security with Anonymous Digital Transaction Framework
item.description: OONI published research on enhancing probe security using principles from David Chaum's anonymous transaction systems, enabling censorship measurement without user identification.

item.title: AI Tools Create New Censorship & Information Access Risks
item.description: AccessNow analysis shows LLMs pose availability risks through data deletion, service disruptions, and content refusals. DeepSeek censors Tiananmen Square topics, while open-source models enable bias removal.

item.title: OONI Documents Telegram Blocks During Kenya Exams, Supports Legal Challenge
item.description: OONI provided technical evidence of Telegram blocking during Kenya's national exams in 2023 and 2024, which is being used in a High Court case challenging internet shutdowns.

item.title: Human Rights Groups Challenge Sandvine Rebrand After Entity List Removal
item.description: Access Now demands AppLogic Networks (formerly Sandvine) prove its surveillance tech reforms after U.S. removed company from Entity List, citing lack of transparency on Egypt exit and failure to remedy DPI abuses.

item.title: OONI Data Shows Iran Blocks Women's Rights Websites Since 2014
item.description: OONI measurement data documents systematic blocking of women's rights websites in Iran since at least 2014, with censorship intensifying despite 2022 Women, Life, Freedom protests.

item.title: OONI Study Reveals Inconsistent DNS Censorship Infrastructure Across French ISPs
item.description: OONI analysis shows France implementing widespread website blocking via DNS manipulation across ISPs, but with inconsistent enforcement that creates escalation risks toward more invasive censorship methods.

item.title: MTN Enabled Gov't Bounty SMS System Linked to Extrajudicial Killings in Congo
item.description: Access Now demands MTN Group investigate how Congo's presidential security forces used MTN's network to send bounty SMS messages offering rewards for informants, amid reports of summary executions.

item.title: Reports: Binance Investigators Found Crypto Flows to Iran, Company Denies Firings
item.description: Unverified reports claim Binance internal investigators discovered substantial crypto flows to Iran through user accounts, though Binance denies firing investigators over the findings.

item.title: 17 Nonprofits Challenge FTC's Retaliatory Investigation of Media Matters
item.description: Coalition led by The Intercept's Press Freedom Defense Fund filed amicus brief to stop FTC's allegedly retaliatory investigation of Media Matters, citing pattern of government intimidation tactics.

item.title: Gabon Blocks Social Media Platforms During Anti-Government Protests
item.description: Gabon's government indefinitely blocked Facebook, WhatsApp, TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube during ongoing protests, with 345+ rights organizations demanding immediate restoration of access.

item.title: Bruce Schneier: AI Arms Races Threaten Democratic Participation and Concentrate Power in Big Tech
item.description: Security expert Bruce Schneier warns that AI is creating adversarial arms races across domains like government-citizen interactions, benefiting Big Tech monopolies while potentially undermining authentic democratic participation.

item.title: Microsoft Shares BitLocker Recovery Keys with FBI, Compromising Disk Encryption
item.description: Microsoft routinely provides BitLocker backup keys to law enforcement like the FBI, undermining the privacy protection of Windows' built-in disk encryption for users storing keys in Microsoft accounts.

item.title: Turkey Throttles Social Media During Political Protests - OONI Documents Technical Evidence
item.description: OONI documented systematic throttling of major social media platforms and blocking of Telegram during March 2025 protests in Turkey following arrest of Istanbul mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu.

item.title: Dave Farber, 'Grandfather of Internet' and EFF Board Member, Dies at 91
item.description: Dave Farber, longest-serving EFF Board member and internet pioneer known as the 'Grandfather of the Internet,' has died at 91, ending decades of advocacy for digital rights and open source protections.

item.title: Cryptographers Find Practical Attacks on Fiat-Shamir Zero-Knowledge Proofs
item.description: New research reveals vulnerabilities in Fiat-Shamir heuristic used in ZK proof systems, showing how attackers can potentially prove false statements in widely-used cryptographic protocols.

item.title: Suriname Blocks Meta Platforms During Cost of Living Protests
item.description: Suriname restricted Facebook, WhatsApp, Instagram and Messenger on government network Telesur during February 2023 protests when demonstrators stormed parliament over economic crisis.

item.title: Cameroon Police Seize Journalist Equipment, Target Sources in Deportation Investigation
item.description: Cameroonian police detained 4 journalists covering secret US migrant deportations, seized their devices, and threatened to access confidential sources by circumventing passwords.

item.title: DHS Using Administrative Subpoenas to Demand User Data from Tech Companies
item.description: Department of Homeland Security is abusing administrative subpoenas to force tech companies like Google to hand over names and locations of users who criticize government online.

item.title: Gabon Shuts Down Social Media Amid Protests, Coalition Demands Restoration
item.description: Gabon's government ordered indefinite social media shutdown during anti-government protests, blocking Facebook, WhatsApp, TikTok, Instagram and YouTube. 345+ human rights orgs demand immediate restoration.

item.title: Angola's Parliament Passes Restrictive NGO Bill Threatening Civil Society Operations
item.description: Angola's parliament approved legislation granting sweeping government control over NGOs, including mandatory authorization, extensive monitoring, financial restrictions, and administrative suspension powers that undermine civil society independence.

item.title: Guinea Blocks Social Media During Protests, Army Called In
item.description: Guinea restricted Facebook, WhatsApp, Instagram, TikTok and other platforms on May 17, 2023 as authorities deployed army to suppress planned protests.

item.title: NetBlocks Documents Systematic Internet Shutdowns During School Exams Across MENA
item.description: NetBlocks documented coordinated internet shutdowns and platform restrictions across Kenya, Iraq, Syria, and Algeria during 2023 school exams, revealing systematic government censorship tactics.

item.title: Wikipedia Defends Volunteer Editors Against Rising Takedown Demands
item.description: Wikimedia Foundation received 664 takedown requests in 2024 (up from 304 over two years a decade ago) but granted only 4, defending community-governed content creation through Section 230 protections.

item.title: Access Now Report: AI Tools Systematically Undermine Data Confidentiality and Privacy
item.description: Major AI platforms like ChatGPT and Meta AI are compromising user privacy through data breaches, lack of encryption, and forced integration that breaks E2EE promises.

item.title: Cameroon Police Seize Journalists' Equipment Investigating US Migrant Deportations
item.description: Cameroonian police detained 4 journalists for 5 hours, seized their phones/laptops while investigating secret US deportation flights, threatening to breach device security and expose confidential sources.

item.title: Iraq Blocks Telegram Nationwide, Kurdistan Region Remains Online
item.description: Iraq restricted Telegram across most of the country citing national security and leaked personal data concerns, while the autonomous Kurdistan region maintains access.

item.title: Microsoft Routinely Shares BitLocker Recovery Keys with FBI
item.description: Proton explains BitLocker encryption following revelations that Microsoft regularly provides BitLocker recovery keys to law enforcement agencies like the FBI.

item.title: EFF Report: How 'Free' Surveillance Tech Creates Accountability Gaps and Mass Data Sharing
item.description: EFF analysis reveals how surveillance vendors use 'free' trials, continued service without funding, and donations to bypass local oversight, creating data pipelines to ICE and eroding civil liberties.

item.title: Citizen Lab Submits UN Reports on Surveillance Tech Enabling Enforced Disappearances
item.description: Citizen Lab researchers submitted two reports to UN bodies detailing how targeted surveillance technology facilitates enforced disappearances and transnational repression of migrants.

item.title: Ethiopia Revokes Independent Media License in Escalating Press Crackdown
item.description: Ethiopian authorities revoked Addis Standard's media license citing vague 'national interest' violations, part of broader crackdown including Reuters accreditation denials and newsroom raids.

item.title: Coalition Demands California Revoke Federal License Plate Reader Permits
item.description: EFF and 25+ organizations demand California revoke permits allowing CBP and DEA to deploy automated license plate readers disguised as traffic barrels along border highways.

item.title: Pakistan Jails Human Rights Lawyers 17 Years for Military Criticism Tweets
item.description: Two Pakistani human rights lawyers sentenced to 17 years under cybercrime law PECA for tweets criticizing military abuses in Balochistan, demonstrating weaponization of digital legislation against dissent.

item.title: Uganda Implements Election Internet Shutdown, Threatens VPN Users with Device Targeting
item.description: Uganda shut down internet and blocked major platforms during 2026 elections despite international pressure. Authorities warn VPN users could be targeted via their devices, highlighting escalating digital surveillance threats.

item.title: OONI and Partners Host Internet Measurement Village at Global Gathering 2025
item.description: Four major internet measurement projects are hosting an 'OMG Village' at Global Gathering 2025 in Portugal to advance censorship monitoring and shutdown detection capabilities.

item.title: Ukraine: 10 Investigative Journalists Surveilled by Criminal Network Tied to Government Officials
item.description: Ukrainian authorities are investigating surveillance of 10 corruption journalists by a criminal network including government officials, discovered during a 15-month anti-corruption probe called 'Operation Midas'.

item.title: Signal Discusses Global Tech Industry US-China Centralization
item.description: Signal's official blog examines how the tech industry is centered in the US and China due to historical material conditions rather than superior talent, touching on implications for global tech development.

item.title: Signal Deploys Faster ORAM for Privacy-Preserving Contact Discovery
item.description: Signal improved their contact discovery system using advanced ORAM techniques and enclaves, laying groundwork for usernames and enhanced phone number privacy controls.

item.title: Iran's Internet Blackout Enabled Mass State Violence and Narrative Control
item.description: Iran's 15-day internet shutdown during 2026 protests enabled unprecedented state violence (reportedly 30,000+ deaths) while regime-aligned diaspora voices filled the information vacuum to control international narratives.

item.title: Discord Rolls Out Mandatory Age Verification Despite Data Breach History
item.description: Discord begins global mandatory age verification requiring government ID or face scans for many users, despite winning EFF's 2025 'Breachie Award' for exposing 70k users' identity documents in a hack.

item.title: Access Now Launches Digital Security Training Series for Civil Society
item.description: Access Now's Digital Security Helpline is hosting webinar series starting March 2026, focusing on spear-phishing defense for activists, journalists, and civil society facing targeted attacks.

item.title: Senegal Arrests Second Journalist for Challenging Official Account of Student Death
item.description: Senegalese authorities arrested freelance commentator Abdou Nguer for questioning prosecutor's denial of torture in student death during university protest crackdown, marking escalating press freedom concerns.
